docs.title: Computus Cloud Beta Docs
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation for Computus Cloud Beta.

Computus Cloud is an independent OpenStack-based cloud platform. This beta environment lets you test our infrastructure directly, with OSIE managing accounts, billing, metering, and workflows, and fully S3-compatible object storage.

This is a closed beta. All usage is metered and subject to strict limits. Your feedback will directly shape the final platform.

docs.title: Beta Endpoints
docs.text: Service Endpoint Notes Beta Portal https://beta.computus.io Main dashboard and management S3 API https://s3.computus.io:8080 Note the :8080 port

docs.title: Beta Limitations (Please Respect These)
docs.text:
Object Storage (S3) - Maximum 200 GB total storage per account - Create credentials via Access → S3/EC2 Credentials in the portal

Compute (Virtual Machines) - Maximum 2 VMs total per account - Available images: Ubuntu, Debian, Rocky Linux - Available flavors: Tiny, Small, Medium only - Public networking: IPv6 only (no public IPv4) - Private IPv4 networking fully supported

Networking - Unlimited private networks and subnets - Full freedom to attach interfaces and experiment

docs.title: Quick Start Guide
docs.text:
1. Sign up / Log in to the Beta Portal
  2. Create S3 Credentials (Access Key + Secret Key)
  3. Create your first Bucket and test at s3.computus.io:8080
  4. Upload an SSH Key
  5. Launch your first VM (select "external net" + your SSH key)
  6. Connect to your VM using its public IPv6 address

docs.title: Main Sections
docs.text:
• Object Storage (S3) — Buckets, credentials, AWS CLI, S3 Browser
  • Compute — Launching VMs, flavors, images, IPv6 access
  • Networking — Private networks and advanced setups
  • Control Panel — Dashboard, billing, projects
  • Troubleshooting — Common beta issues

We want your feedback! Use the Support Requests section in the beta portal to report bugs, share what worked well, suggest improvements, or describe interesting private networking setups you build.

Thank you for helping test Computus Cloud!

— The Computus Team

docs.title: Creating a VM
docs.text:
# Creating a VM (Beta)

Follow these steps to launch your first virtual machine.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

1. Log in to [https://beta.computus.io](https://beta.computus.io)

2. In the left menu, go to **Compute → Instances**

3. Click **Launch Instance** or **Create VM**

### Required Settings:

- **Name**: Give your VM a meaningful name

- **Image**: Choose one of:

- Ubuntu (latest)

- Debian (latest)

- Rocky Linux (latest)

- **Flavor**:

- Tiny

- Small

- Medium

- **SSH Key**: Select the SSH key you previously uploaded (required)

- **Network**: Select **external net** (this is the default — very important)

- **Security Group**: Use the default one for now

4. Click **Launch** / **Create**

After a few minutes, your VM should show as **Active** with a public **IPv6** address.

## How to Connect

Use SSH with your private key:

```bash

ssh -i \~/.ssh/your_private_key user@your-ipv6-address

docs.title: Available Flavors
docs.text:
---

### 3. compute/flavors.md

```markdown

# Available Flavors (Beta)

Only three flavors are available during the beta:

| Flavor | vCPUs | RAM | Disk | Recommended Use |

|---------|-------|--------|--------|-----------------|

| **Tiny** | 1 | 1 GB | 20 GB | Testing, light services |

| **Small** | 2 | 2 GB | 40 GB | Small websites, development |

| **Medium** | 4 | 4 GB | 60 GB | Medium workloads, databases |

These are the only sizes available. Larger flavors will be added after beta.

---

**Next:** [Available Images](images.md)

docs.title: IPv6 Access Only
docs.text:
# IPv6 Access Only (Beta)

During the beta, all virtual machines receive a **public IPv6 address** automatically. There are **no public IPv4 addresses** available.

## What You Get

- One public IPv6 address per VM (on "external net")

- Full outbound and inbound IPv6 connectivity

- Private IPv4 address on the internal network

## Connecting to Your VM

You must connect using the IPv6 address via SSH. Most modern operating systems and networks support IPv6.

**Example:**

```bash

ssh ubuntu@2607:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx::xxxx

docs.title: Available Images
docs.text:
# Available Images (Beta)

The following official images are available:

- **Ubuntu** — Latest stable version (recommended for most users)

- **Debian** — Latest stable version

- **Rocky Linux** — Latest stable version (RHEL compatible)

These are clean, minimal images with cloud-init support for SSH key injection.

**Note:** Custom image upload is not available during the beta.

---

**Next:** [SSH Keys (Required)](ssh-keys.md)

docs.title: Instances Overview
docs.text:
# Instances Overview (Beta)

The Compute section allows you to create and manage virtual machines on Computus Cloud Beta.

## Beta Compute Limits

- Maximum **2 virtual machines** total per account

- Only **Tiny**, **Small**, and **Medium** flavors are available

- Available images: **Ubuntu**, **Debian**, and **Rocky Linux**

- Public access: **IPv6 only** (no public IPv4 addresses)

- Private IPv4 networking is fully supported

## Key Points

- You must upload at least one SSH key before launching a VM

- All VMs are billed hourly while running (metered only — no real charges in beta)

- VMs receive a public IPv6 address automatically when launched on the external network

**Tip:** We are very interested in your feedback on performance, boot times, and networking behavior.

---

**Next:** [Creating a VM (Beta)](creating-a-vm.md)

docs.title: Reboot, Stop & Resize
docs.text:
---

### 7. compute/reboot-stop-resize.md

```markdown

# Reboot, Stop \& Resize

## Available Actions

| Action | Description |

|------------|-----------|

| **Reboot** | Soft or Hard reboot of the VM |

| **Stop / Shut Off** | Power off the VM (still billed while stopped) |

| **Start** | Power on a stopped VM |

| **Resize** | Change flavor (Tiny ↔ Small ↔ Medium) |

**Note:** Resize may require the VM to be stopped first.

## Recommendations

- Stop your VMs when not in use to reduce compute usage (even though no real charges in beta)

- Always make sure you have important data backed up before resizing

---

You have now completed the full **Compute** section.

docs.title: SSH Keys
docs.text:
# SSH Keys (Required)

You **must** upload at least one SSH public key before you can create any virtual machine.

## How to Add an SSH Key

1. Go to **Access → SSH Keys** in the dashboard

2. Click **Create SSH Key** or **Add Key**

3. Give it a clear name (e.g. "My Laptop Key")

4. Paste the content of your **public key**:

- Usually \~/.ssh/id\_ed25519.pub or \~/.ssh/id\_rsa.pub

5. Click **Save**

## Tips

- Use Ed25519 keys when possible (more secure and faster)

- You can add multiple keys

- The key will be automatically injected into new VMs

**Important:** You cannot add or change the SSH key after the VM is created.

---

**Next:** [IPv6 Access Only](floating-ips.md)

docs.title: Accessing the Portal
docs.text: The Computus Cloud Beta is managed entirely through the OSIE Portal.

docs.title: Beta Portal URL
docs.text: https://beta.computus.io

docs.title: How to Access
docs.text:
1. Go to https://beta.computus.io
  2. Enter your email address and password
  3. Complete any required MFA or security checks (if enabled)
  4. You will be redirected to the Computus Dashboard

docs.title: Supported Browsers
docs.text:
• Google Chrome (recommended)
  • Mozilla Firefox
  • Microsoft Edge

Note: The portal is fully responsive but works best on desktop browsers.

docs.title: Logging Out
docs.text:
Click your username in the top right corner and select Logout.

Next: Dashboard Overview

docs.title: API Keys & Credentials
docs.text: This section is critical for using the platform.

docs.title: 1. S3 Credentials (Object Storage)
docs.text:
Path: Access → S3 Credentials

How to create:

  1. Go to Access → S3 Credentials
  2. Click Create Credentials
  3. Give it a description (e.g. "My Backup Tool")
  4. Click Create
  5. Immediately copy and save the Access Key and Secret Key securely

These are used with the S3 endpoint https://s3.computus.io:8080

docs.title: 2. SSH Keys (for Virtual Machines)
docs.text:
Path: Access → SSH Keys

How to create:

  1. Go to Access → SSH Keys
  2. Click Create SSH Key
  3. Give it a name
  4. Paste your public key (e.g. content of id_ed25519.pub or id_rsa.pub)
  5. Click Save

You must upload at least one SSH key before launching any VM.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Never share your Secret Key
  • Create separate credentials for different tools or applications
  • Rotate credentials periodically
  • Delete unused credentials

Next: Dashboard Overview

docs.title: Billing Overview
docs.text: All resource usage is metered and displayed in the dashboard.

docs.title: Where to Find Billing
docs.text: Click Billing in the main left menu of the dashboard.

docs.title: What You Will See
docs.text:
• Current usage and estimated costs
  • Breakdown by Compute, Object Storage, and Networking
  • Running balance (this may show as negative during beta)
  • Invoices and payment history
  • Added payment methods

docs.title: Important Beta Billing Information
docs.text:
• No actual charges will be made during the beta period.
  • Your account balance may go into the negative as you use resources — this is expected and normal.
  • Billing enforcement is turned off.
  • You will not be suspended or banned for negative balance or exceeding soft limits.
  • The metering system is active so we can test real-world usage patterns.

We recommend checking the billing page regularly to see how different actions affect usage.

Next: API Keys & Credentials

docs.title: Dashboard Overview
docs.text: The Computus Cloud dashboard is your central hub for managing all resources in the beta.

docs.title: Main Sections
docs.text: Menu Item Description Dashboard Overview of your usage, active VMs, storage quota, and quick actions Compute Manage virtual machines (Instances) Networking Create and manage networks, subnets, routers, and security groups Object Storage S3 credentials and storage overview Access SSH Keys and S3 Credentials Billing Usage, invoices, payments, and credits Projects Project and tenant management Support Submit feedback and support requests

docs.title: Key Dashboard Widgets (Beta)
docs.text:
• Current VM count (out of 2 allowed)
  • S3 storage usage (out of 200 GB)
  • Active instances and their IPv6 addresses
  • Recent billing summary

Tip: Bookmark https://beta.computus.io for quick access.

Next: Projects and Tenants

docs.title: Projects and Tenants
docs.text: During the beta, Computus Cloud uses a single-project model for simplicity.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• When you sign up, a project is automatically created for you.
  • All your resources (VMs, networks, S3 buckets, etc.) belong to this single project.
  • You cannot create additional projects during the beta.

docs.title: Project Details
docs.text:
You can view your project information by going to Projects in the left menu.

Typical information shown:

  • Project ID
  • Project Name
  • Creation date
  • Current quotas and usage

Note: Multi-project support will be added after the beta.

Next: Billing Overview

docs.title: How to Create a Beta Account
docs.text:
1. Go to https://beta.computus.io
  2. Click Sign Up / Register
  3. Enter your real email address (this is required for verification)
  4. Fill in the billing information (you can use fake card details — no charges will be processed during beta)
  5. Choose a strong password
  6. Complete registration
  7. Verify your email by clicking the link sent to your inbox

docs.title: Important Beta Signup Notes
docs.text:
• A valid payment method must be added to complete signup, but no real charges will occur.
  • You can use placeholder/fake billing details.
  • Once verified, you can immediately start using the platform.

Next: First Login to beta.computus.io

docs.title: Beta Limitations & Rules
docs.text: Please read this page carefully.

docs.title: Current Beta Limits
docs.text: Resource Limit Notes Virtual Machines 2 VMs total per account Cannot be increased VM Flavors Tiny, Small, Medium only — Operating Systems Ubuntu, Debian, Rocky Linux — Public Networking IPv6 only No public IPv4 Object Storage 200 GB total Hard limit across all buckets Private Networks Unlimited Experiment freely

docs.title: Billing During Beta
docs.text:
• Usage is fully metered and visible in the Billing section
  • Your account balance may go negative — this is normal and expected
  • No real charges will be processed
  • Billing enforcement is disabled — you will not be suspended for negative balance or usage

docs.title: Important Rules
docs.text:
• Do not attempt to bypass the 2-VM limit
  • Report any bugs or unexpected behavior through Support
  • Your feedback is extremely valuable

Next: Account Signup (Beta)

docs.title: Email verification
docs.text:
# First Login to beta.computus.io

After verifying your email:

1. Go to [https://beta.computus.io](https://beta.computus.io)

2. Log in with your email and password

3. You will land on the main dashboard

## Recommended First Actions After Login

1. **Create S3 Credentials**

- Go to **Access → S3/EC2 Credentials**

- Click **Create Credentials**

- Save your **Access Key** and **Secret Key** securely

2. **Upload an SSH Public Key**

- Go to **Access → SSH Keys**

- Click **Create SSH Key** and paste your public key

3. **Review Your Limits**

- Check current usage under Billing

4. Start testing:

- Create your first S3 bucket

- Launch your first VM (max 2 total)

## Dashboard Tips

- Use the left sidebar to navigate between Compute, Networking, Object Storage, etc.

- Monitor your usage regularly in the Billing section

- Use the Support Requests page to send feedback

---

You have now completed the Getting Started section!

docs.title: First Login to beta.computus.io
docs.text:
After verifying your email:

  1. Go to https://beta.computus.io
  2. Log in with your email and password
  3. You will land on the main dashboard

docs.title: Recommended First Actions After Login
docs.text:
1. Create S3 Credentials
  2. Go to Access → S3/EC2 Credentials
  3. Click Create Credentials

  4. Save your Access Key and Secret Key securely

  5. Upload an SSH Public Key

  6. Go to Access → SSH Keys

  7. Click Create SSH Key and paste your public key

  8. Review Your Limits

  9. Check current usage under Billing

  10. Start testing:

  11. Create your first S3 bucket
  12. Launch your first VM (max 2 total)

docs.title: Dashboard Tips
docs.text:
• Use the left sidebar to navigate between Compute, Networking, Object Storage, etc.
  • Monitor your usage regularly in the Billing section
  • Use the Support Requests page to send feedback

You have now completed the Getting Started section!

docs.title: Platform Overview
docs.text:
Computus Cloud Beta consists of two main access points:

Component URL Purpose Beta Portal https://beta.computus.io Web dashboard, VM management, billing, SSH keys, S3 credentials S3 API Endpoint https://s3.computus.io:8080 Object storage (buckets & files)

docs.title: Core Technology Stack
docs.text:
• OpenStack — powers compute, networking, and storage
  • OSIE — handles user accounts, billing, metering, and portal workflows
  • Swift (with S3 compatibility) — provides Amazon S3-compatible object storage

docs.title: Beta Architecture Summary
docs.text:
• You manage everything through the web portal at beta.computus.io
  • VMs are launched on our OpenStack cluster
  • Public access to VMs is via IPv6 only
  • Private IPv4 networks are fully available for internal communication between VMs
  • Object storage is accessed directly via the S3 API

Next: Beta Limitations & Rules

docs.title: What is Computus Cloud Beta?
docs.text:
Computus Cloud is an independent cloud computing platform built on OpenStack, designed to provide reliable virtual machines, private networking, and S3-compatible object storage.

This Beta version allows early testers to try the platform before public launch. The beta runs on our production-grade OpenStack deployment with OSIE handling user accounts, billing, metering, and service workflows.

docs.title: Key Features in Beta
docs.text:
• Virtual machines (limited to 2 per account)
  • S3-compatible object storage (max 200 GB)
  • Full private networking capabilities
  • IPv6 public access
  • Pay-as-you-go metering through OSIE

docs.title: Why We're Running a Beta
docs.text:
We want real users to test the platform, discover edge cases, and give feedback on usability, performance, and features before opening it to everyone.

Your participation helps us improve stability, documentation, and the overall experience.

Next: Platform Overview

docs.title: Cannot SSH to VM
docs.text:
• Confirm you are using the public IPv6 address
  • Make sure the correct SSH key was selected during VM creation
  • Check Security Group allows port 22

docs.title: No Internet Inside Private Networks
docs.text:
• Create a router and set gateway to external net
  • Ensure the VM interface is attached to the correct network

docs.title: Private IPv4 not working between VMs
docs.text:
• Verify both VMs are attached to the same private network/subnet
  • Check firewall rules inside the VMs

Report any unusual behavior through the dashboard Support.

docs.title: Networks Overview (Beta)
docs.text: Computus Cloud Beta gives you full control over networking. You can create private networks and attach them to your VMs as needed.

docs.title: Beta Networking Highlights
docs.text:
• Public access: IPv6 only (automatic when using "external net")
  • Private IPv4 networking: Fully supported and unlimited
  • You can create as many private networks and subnets as you want
  • We are especially interested in creative private networking setups during beta

docs.title: Main Networking Components
docs.text:
• Private Networks — Isolated Layer 2 networks
  • Subnets — IP address ranges inside networks
  • Routers — For connecting networks and enabling outbound access
  • Security Groups — Firewall rules for your VM

Next: Private Networks & Experiments

docs.title: Private Networks & Experiments (Beta)
docs.text: This is one of the most interesting areas for beta testing.

docs.title: How to Create a Private Network
docs.text:
1. Go to Networking → Networks in the dashboard
  2. Click Create Network
  3. Give it a name (e.g. internal-app-net, database-tier)
  4. Click Create

You can then create subnets inside the network and attach interfaces to your VMs.

docs.title: What We Want You to Test
docs.text:
• Multiple private networks between your 2 VMs
  • Different subnet configurations
  • Inter-VM communication over private IPv4
  • Any creative setups (web tier + db tier, etc.)

Please share your experiments and any issues via the Support section in the dashboard.

Next: Subnets & Routers

docs.title: Creating a Subnet
docs.text:
1. Go to the network you created
  2. Click Create Subnet
  3. Set:
  4. Subnet Name
  5. Network Address (CIDR) — e.g. 192.168.10.0/24
  6. Gateway IP (optional)

docs.title: Creating a Router
docs.text:
Routers allow traffic between networks or to the external network.

  1. Routers will not be available during this Beta

Note: During beta, IPv6 is the primary public connectivity method.

Next: Security Groups

docs.title: Security Groups
docs.text: Security Groups act as virtual firewalls for your VMs.

docs.title: Default Security Group
docs.text:
• Allows all outbound traffic
  • Allows SSH (port 22) from anywhere (IPv6)
  • You can modify or create new ones

docs.title: Recommended for Beta
docs.text:
Start with the default group. You can open additional ports (HTTP, HTTPS, custom ports) as needed for your tests.

Next: Common Networking Issues

docs.title: Creating Access & Secret Keys
docs.text: You need an Access Key and Secret Key to use the S3 API.

docs.title: How to Create Credentials
docs.text:
1. Log in to https://beta.computus.io
  2. In the left menu, go to Access → S3 Credentials
  3. Click Create Credentials / Create S3 Key
  4. Enter a description (e.g. "AWS CLI Key" or "Cyberduck")
  5. Click Create
  6. Immediately copy the following:
  7. Access Key
  8. Secret Key

Save these securely. The Secret Key will not be shown again.

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• You can create multiple key pairs
  • Each key works with all your buckets
  • Rotate or delete keys regularly for security

Next: Creating Buckets

docs.title: Bucket Policies & CORS
docs.text:
# Bucket Policies \& CORS

Advanced bucket policies and CORS configuration are supported.

For most beta testing, the default private settings are sufficient.

If you need public access or specific CORS rules (e.g. for web applications), you can apply them using AWS CLI or your S3 client.

---

**Next:** [Troubleshooting S3 Access](troubleshooting-s3-access.md))

docs.title: Creating Buckets
docs.text:
# Creating Buckets

## How to Create a Bucket

You can create buckets using any S3 client (recommended) or through the S3 API.

### Using AWS CLI (Example)

```bash

aws s3 mb s3://my-first-bucket \

--endpoint-url https://s3.computus.io:8080

docs.title: S3 Object Storage Overview (Beta)
docs.text: Computus Cloud provides fully S3-compatible object storage, perfect for backups, media storage, application data, and more.

docs.title: Beta Limits
docs.text:
• 200 GB maximum total storage per account
  • Usage is metered daily
  • No enforcement — you can go over temporarily, but please stay close to the limit

docs.title: Quick Start Summary
docs.text:
1. Create S3 credentials in the dashboard
  2. Create a bucket
  3. Test with AWS CLI, S3 Browser, Cyberduck, or any S3 client

Endpoint: https://s3.computus.io:8080

Next: S3 Endpoint

docs.title: Endpoint Details
docs.text:
• URL: https://s3.computus.io:8080
  • Port: 8080 (important — do not omit it)
  • Protocol: HTTPS
  • Compatible with: AWS CLI, s3cmd, Cyberduck, S3 Browser, rclone, boto3, etc.

Note: This is different from the production endpoint. Use :8080 during beta.

Next: Creating Access & Secret Keys

---

**Next:** [Creating Access \& Secret Keys](access-keys-and-secret-keys.md)

docs.title: Common Issue: SignatureDoesNotMatch
docs.text:
• Make sure you are using the correct endpoint: https://s3.computus.io:8080
  • Check that your Access Key and Secret Key are copied correctly (no extra spaces)
  • Use --endpoint-url flag with every AWS CLI command

docs.title: Connection Refused / Timeout
docs.text:
• Confirm you are using port :8080
  • Check your firewall / network supports IPv6 (some corporate networks block it)

docs.title: Access Denied
docs.text:
• Verify the bucket name is correct
  • Check your bucket policies

Feel free to report any other issues via Support in the dashboard.

docs.title: Configure AWS CLI
docs.text:
Run this command and fill in the details:

```bash aws configure --profile computus

aws --profile computus \ --endpoint-url https://s3.computus.io:8080 \ s3 ls

aws --profile computus \ --endpoint-url https://s3.computus.io:8080 \ s3 mb s3://my-first-bucket

aws --profile computus \ --endpoint-url https://s3.computus.io:8080 \ s3 cp file.txt s3://my-first-bucket/

docs.title: ✅ 4. using-s3-browser-and-cyberduck.md
docs.text:
(Fixed from :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3})

```md

docs.title: Recommended Tools
docs.text:
• S3 Browser (Windows) — simple and free
  • Cyberduck (Windows / macOS) — user friendly
  • rclone — great for scripting

docs.title: Cyberduck Setup Example
docs.text:
1. Open Cyberduck
  2. Click Open Connection
  3. Choose Amazon S3
  4. Server: s3.computus.io
  5. Port: 8080
  6. Access Key ID: your key
  7. Secret Key: your key
  8. Click Connect

Next: Bucket Policies & CORS

docs.title: High-Level Architecture
docs.text:
# High-Level Architecture

Computus Cloud Beta is built on a modern OpenStack-based infrastructure designed for reliability and ease of use.

## Architecture Overview

```text

[ Your Browser ]

|

v

https://beta.computus.io

(Computus Dashboard)

|

v

OpenStack Services

├── Nova (Compute)

├── Neutron (Networking)

├── Glance (Images)

├── Keystone (Identity)

└── Swift (Object Storage with S3 compatibility)

|

v

s3.computus.io:8080

docs.title: OpenStack Services
docs.text:
---

### 2. platform-architecture/openstack-services.md

```markdown

# OpenStack Integration

Computus Cloud is powered by **OpenStack**, one of the most widely used open-source cloud platforms.

## What This Means for You

- Full OpenStack API compatibility (advanced users)

- Standard OpenStack concepts (flavors, images, security groups, etc.)

- Proven, enterprise-grade infrastructure

- S3-compatible object storage layer on top of Swift

## Beta Specifics

- Limited to 2 VMs and 200 GB S3 storage

- Public IPv6 only (no IPv4 floating IPs yet)

- Single project/tenant model

- OSIE-style dashboard on top for simplified user experience

This architecture allows us to rapidly improve and expand the platform based on your beta feedback.

---

**End of Platform Architecture section.**

docs.title: Endpoint Reference (Beta)
docs.text:
# Endpoint Reference (Beta)

## Main Endpoints

| Service | URL | Notes |

|----------------------|------------------------------------|-------|

| **Beta Dashboard** | https://beta.computus.io | Main web portal |

| **S3 Object Storage**| https://s3.computus.io:8080 | **Port 8080 required** |

## Important Notes

- Always use https://s3.computus.io:8080 for S3 operations during beta

- No public IPv4 addresses — use IPv6 for VM access

docs.title: FAQ (Beta)
docs.text:
# Frequently Asked Questions (Beta)

**Q: Will I be charged?**

A: No. Billing enforcement is disabled. Your balance may go negative — this is expected.

**Q: How many VMs can I run?**

A: Maximum 2 VMs total.

**Q: Can I use public IPv4?**

A: Not during beta. Use IPv6 only for public access.

**Q: What is the S3 limit?**

A: 200 GB total across all buckets.

**Q: How do I give feedback?**

A: Email support@computus.io with your feedback

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
# Glossary

- **Flavor** — Virtual machine size (Tiny, Small, Medium)

- **Image** — Operating system template (Ubuntu, Debian, Rocky Linux)

- **External Net** — The network that provides public IPv6 access

- **Project/Tenant** — Your isolated environment (single project in beta)

- **S3 Credentials** — Access Key + Secret Key pair for object storage

docs.title: Support & Feedback
docs.text:
# Frequently Asked Questions (Beta)

**Q: Will I be charged?**

A: No. Billing enforcement is disabled. Your balance may go negative — this is expected.

**Q: How many VMs can I run?**

A: Maximum 2 VMs total.

**Q: Can I use public IPv4?**

A: Not during beta. Use IPv6 only for public access.

**Q: What is the S3 limit?**

A: 200 GB total across all buckets.

**Q: How do I give feedback?**

A: Use the **Support** section in the dashboard.

docs.title: Bucket Access Denied
docs.text:
---

### 4. troubleshooting/bucket-access-denied.md

```markdown

# Bucket Access Denied

## Possible Causes \& Fixes

- Using the wrong Access Key / Secret Key

- Trying to access a bucket that doesn't exist

- Bucket name contains uppercase letters or invalid characters

- Using the wrong endpoint (:8080 is required)

**Quick Test:**

```bash

aws s3 ls --endpoint-url https://s3.computus.io:8080

docs.title: Cannot Log In
docs.text:
# Cannot Log In to beta.computus.io

## Common Solutions

1. **Email Verification**

- Check your spam/junk folder for the verification email

- Request a new verification link from the login page

2. **Password Reset**

- Use the **Forgot Password** link on the login page

3. **Browser Issues**

- Try incognito/private mode

- Clear cache and cookies

- Try a different browser (Chrome recommended)

4. **Account Not Approved**

- Beta access may require manual approval — contact support if you signed up but can't log in

---

**Next:** [VM Build Failed](vm-build-failed.md)

docs.title: S3 Issues
docs.text:
# S3 Error: SignatureDoesNotMatch

This is the most common S3 error during beta.

## Fixes

1. **Use the correct endpoint**

```bash

https://s3.computus.io:8080

docs.title: VM Build Failed
docs.text:
# VM Build Failed

## Common Reasons

- No SSH key uploaded before launching the instance

- Did not select **"external net"** as the network

- Selected a flavor or image that is not available in beta

- Reached the limit of 2 VMs

## How to Fix

1. Go to **Access → SSH Keys** and upload a public key

2. Delete any failed instance

3. Launch again, making sure to:

- Choose Ubuntu/Debian/Rocky

- Choose Tiny/Small/Medium flavor

- Select **external net**

- Select your SSH key

Check the instance logs in the dashboard for more details.
